findings.evidence: `rg -n "LiftCondition|liftCondition_iff_range_le" code/TauCeti/Topology/Homotopy/Covering.lean` shows `@[expose] def IsCoveringMap.LiftCondition` at line 44 and the intended restatement at lines 51-55.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from `LiftCondition` and rely on the characteristic iff lemma for users who need the range-inclusion form.
findings.issue: `IsCoveringMap.LiftCondition` is marked `@[expose]`, exposing the raw subgroup-inclusion body even though the public API already provides `liftCondition_iff_range_le`.

findings.evidence: Lines 51-55 define the iff as `Iff.rfl` with no `@[simp]`; the roadmap target at `roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/UniversalCovers/README.md:100-106` asks for the precise subgroup-inclusion condition.
findings.fix: Mark `liftCondition_iff_range_le` as `@[simp]` after removing body exposure.
findings.issue: The normal-form characterization `IsCoveringMap.liftCondition_iff_range_le` has no automation annotation, so `simp` will not rewrite `LiftCondition` to the precise Mathlib subgroup condition once the body is hidden.

summary: The wrapper API is present, but it exposes the definition body and leaves the characteristic restatement out of simp, so downstream use depends on unfolding rather than the intended public lemma.
